meta_title: Roofing in The Acreage, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in The Acreage, FL. CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile. Palm Beach County licensed. Get a free quote today!
h1: Trusted Roofing Replacement & Repair in The Acreage, Florida
intro:
The Acreage is one of Palm Beach County's most distinctive communities — a sprawling, semi-rural landscape where families on large lots in neighborhoods like Indian Trail, Hamlin Boulevard, and along Seminole Pratt Whitney Road have built real roots. Homes here average around $430,000, and for most residents, that home represents the single largest investment they'll ever make. At the center of protecting that investment is something that often gets overlooked until it's too late: the roof. Whether your home was built in the 1980s before modern hurricane codes took effect, or constructed more recently, the roof you're living under is your first and most critical line of defense against South Florida's unpredictable and often violent weather.

The Acreage sits approximately 20 miles inland from the Atlantic coast, and while that distance provides some buffer from direct storm surge, it does absolutely nothing to protect against high winds, wind-driven rain, or the kind of sustained tropical force that hurricanes routinely deliver across Palm Beach County. This zip code, 33470, falls within a 140 mph wind design zone under the Florida Building Code — meaning roofing systems installed here must meet rigorous structural and uplift-resistance standards. Homes with aging asphalt shingles, cracked clay tiles, or improperly fastened decking are extremely vulnerable when storms like Irma or Wilma make their presence known.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we understand the unique demands that The Acreage places on roofing systems. The large lot sizes, mature tree canopies, and mix of architectural styles — from traditional block homes near Acreage Community Park to newer builds tucked along winding roads off Hamlin Boulevard — mean there is no one-size-fits-all solution. Our team brings 60+ years of combined roofing experience specifically in Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we've completed projects on 500+ homes across the region. We know the permit process through the Palm Beach County Building Division inside and out, and we only install roofing systems that meet or exceed current Florida Building Code requirements for this wind zone.

section_why.h2: Why The Acreage Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roof
section_why.body:
When you're investing in a full roof replacement or a critical repair, the contractor you choose matters just as much as the materials they install. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has earned the reputation as 'The Kings of Service' across Palm Beach County not by cutting corners, but by doing the opposite — providing 100% in-house installation on every single project, no subcontractors, no third-party labor crews showing up at your home with unfamiliar faces and divided loyalties. When our team pulls into your driveway in The Acreage, you're getting the same experienced, vetted professionals who have been installing roofs for us for years.

This matters especially in communities like The Acreage, where homes tend to have more complex rooflines than urban tract housing, larger square footages, and sometimes unconventional architectural details that require real on-the-job problem-solving. Our crews have worked on everything from simple gable roofs to complex hip designs with multiple valleys and dormers. We handle the full scope: tear-off, deck inspection and repair, underlayment installation, flashing, and final roofing system installation — all under one licensed and insured team.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing holds an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and has completed roofing projects on more than 500 homes across South Florida. We are fully licensed and insured in the state of Florida, and we pull all required permits through the Palm Beach County Building Division on your behalf. This protects you legally, ensures your new roof is inspected to code, and — critically — validates your wind mitigation report for insurance purposes. Many homeowners in neighborhoods like Indian Trail have saved hundreds of dollars annually on their homeowners insurance simply because we documented and reported the right installation details.

We also believe that a great roof shouldn't be financially out of reach. That's why we offer flexible financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available. Our project consultants will walk you through product options, cost breakdowns, and financing scenarios so you can make a confident, informed decision — with no pressure and no surprises.

section_history.h2: Storm History in The Acreage: What the Last 30 Years Has Taught Us
section_history.body:
The Acreage and the broader western Palm Beach County corridor have been tested repeatedly by Atlantic hurricanes over the past three decades. Since 1990, the area has experienced six significant storm events, with three leaving particularly lasting impressions on local homeowners. Hurricane Frances struck in September 2004, battering Palm Beach County with sustained winds and prolonged rainfall that exposed vulnerabilities in roofing systems throughout the region — especially homes with older, single-nail fastened shingles and deteriorated underlayments. Just over a year later, Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 delivered one of the fastest-moving and most destructive hurricane passes in Florida history, with wind gusts that stripped shingles, cracked tiles, and tore off ridge caps across thousands of homes in communities like those surrounding Indian Trails Sports Complex.

Then came Hurricane Irma in September 2017 — a slow-moving giant that drenched and battered every corner of South Florida for hours. Irma was particularly instructive for communities inland like The Acreage because it demonstrated that distance from the coast is no guarantee of safety. Wind speeds in this area during Irma reached levels sufficient to cause widespread roofing damage, particularly to homes built before the sweeping 2002 revisions to the Florida Building Code. Approximately 20% of homes in The Acreage predate 1994 — meaning they were built before the post-Hurricane Andrew code reforms that fundamentally changed how roofs must be constructed and attached in South Florida. If your home falls into that pre-1994 category, or if you haven't had a professional roof inspection since Irma, the time to act is before the next named storm enters the Gulf or Atlantic — not after.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Roofing in The Acreage and Palm Beach County
section_code.body:
The Acreage falls under the jurisdiction of the Palm Beach County Building Division, which enforces the Florida Building Code — one of the most rigorous residential construction codes in the United States. While The Acreage is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), which applies to Miami-Dade and Broward counties south of a designated boundary, the area is still classified within a 140 mph wind design zone. This classification has significant implications for every roofing system installed here. All roofing materials, fastening patterns, and underlayment systems must be rated and installed to withstand wind uplift forces consistent with 140 mph exposure.

Under the current Florida Building Code (FBC), a full roof replacement in The Acreage requires a permit to be pulled through the Palm Beach County Building Division prior to any work beginning. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles this permitting process in full on every project — we prepare the documentation, submit the application, coordinate the inspection schedule, and ensure your final roof passes inspection without any action items on the homeowner. This is not something you want to leave to a contractor who suggests 'working without a permit to save time.' An unpermitted roof replacement can void your homeowners insurance policy, complicate a future home sale, and leave you legally exposed if a storm damages your home and an adjuster discovers the work was done outside of code.

For Palm Beach County specifically, roofing systems must also comply with product approval requirements — meaning the shingles, tiles, and underlayments used must carry a Florida Product Approval number showing they've been tested and certified for this wind zone. Every product we install at Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, from CertainTeed Landmark shingles to West Lake concrete and clay tile systems, carries the appropriate Florida Product Approval and is installed per manufacturer specifications and FBC guidelines. After installation, we also document the key metrics needed for a wind mitigation inspection, which can unlock significant discounts on your homeowners insurance premium.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Landmark Shingles and West Lake Tile: Premium Roofing for The Acreage
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two leading roofing systems that are ideally matched to the needs of homeowners in The Acreage: CertainTeed architectural shingles from the industry-renowned Landmark series, and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. These aren't entry-level products — they represent the upper tier of residential roofing materials available in the Florida market, and they're specifically engineered to perform in high-wind environments like Palm Beach County's 140 mph wind zone.

CertainTeed's Landmark series is one of the most trusted architectural shingle products in North America. These are heavyweight, dual-layered laminate shingles that mimic the dimensional appearance of wood shake while delivering dramatically superior wind and impact resistance. Landmark shingles carry a Class A fire rating, a 110 mph wind warranty as standard, and are available in enhanced versions rated up to 130 mph. For The Acreage's predominantly residential streetscapes — where curb appeal matters and home values reflect quality construction — Landmark shingles offer an exceptional combination of aesthetics and performance. They're available in a wide range of color profiles that complement both traditional Florida block homes and newer construction styles throughout neighborhoods like Indian Trail and Hamlin Boulevard.

For homeowners who prefer the classic look of tile — a style deeply embedded in South Florida's architectural identity — West Lake concrete and clay tile systems are the premium choice. West Lake manufactures tile products specifically for the Florida market, with profiles and installation systems engineered for high-wind performance. Concrete tile offers exceptional durability and longevity, often lasting 40 to 50 years with proper maintenance, while clay tile provides unmatched thermal performance and a distinctive aesthetic that can significantly increase resale value. Both tile systems are Florida Product Approved and installed by our in-house crews using code-compliant fastening methods appropriate for The Acreage's wind design requirements.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in The Acreage, and Is It Worth It?
section_cost.body:
Roofing costs in The Acreage vary depending on the size of your home, the roofing system selected, the condition of the existing decking, and the complexity of your roofline. For a typical home in the 33470 zip code — which tends to run larger than average given the lot sizes and family-oriented nature of the community — a full architectural shingle replacement can range from approximately $12,000 to $22,000, while tile systems typically start higher due to material costs and the additional structural considerations involved. These are investments, not just expenses, and Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is committed to helping you understand exactly what you're getting for every dollar spent.

With an average home value of approximately $430,000 in The Acreage, a high-quality new roof represents one of the highest-return home improvements you can make. Studies consistently show that a new roof recovers between 60% and 80% of its cost in added home value, and in a wind-zone market like Palm Beach County, a properly documented hurricane-rated roof can have an even more dramatic effect on both resale value and insurance costs. Buyers in this market increasingly ask for wind mitigation reports and roof age documentation before making offers, and a new CertainTeed or West Lake roof gives you a compelling, verifiable answer.

For homeowners concerned about upfront costs, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified applicants. We work with multiple financing partners to find terms that fit your budget, whether you're planning a full replacement now or need to address storm damage quickly before the next rainy season. Combined with the insurance savings that typically follow a qualifying roof installation, many of our clients in The Acreage find that their net monthly cost — after factoring in reduced insurance premiums — is lower than expected.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof in The Acreage Can Lower Your Homeowners Insurance Premium
section_insurance.body:
Palm Beach County homeowners pay some of the highest homeowners insurance premiums in the United States, driven largely by the region's exposure to hurricane risk. The good news for residents of The Acreage is that a new, properly installed hurricane-rated roof is one of the most effective ways to reduce those premiums — sometimes significantly. Florida law requires insurance carriers to offer discounts to homeowners who can demonstrate wind mitigation features on their property, and a qualifying new roof is the single biggest factor in that calculation.

After Premier Impact Windows & Roofing completes your roofing installation, you are entitled to request a wind mitigation inspection from a licensed Florida inspector. That report documents your roof's age, covering type, deck attachment method, and roof-to-wall connection strength. When submitted to your insurance carrier, a strong wind mitigation report can result in annual premium discounts ranging from several hundred dollars to over a thousand dollars per year, depending on your carrier and coverage level. Common insurers active in Palm Beach County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and others — all recognize and price these discounts into their policies.

Beyond the wind mitigation credits, a new roof also eliminates the surcharges many carriers now impose on homes with roofs older than 15 to 20 years. Some insurers in Florida have moved toward non-renewal of policies on older roofs altogether — a growing trend that has caught many homeowners off guard. By replacing your roof now with a Florida Product Approved system installed to current code, you protect not just your home from storm damage, but your insurability itself. Our team will walk you through exactly which installation details to highlight in your wind mitigation report to maximize the discounts available to you through your specific carrier.

faqs.q: How do I know if my roof in The Acreage needs to be replaced versus repaired?
faqs.a: The best way to determine this is with a professional inspection. Signs that a full replacement may be needed include shingles older than 20 years, visible granule loss in gutters, multiple areas of lifted or missing shingles, soft spots on the decking, or recurring leaks after repeated repairs. Homes in The Acreage built before 1994 are especially likely to have aging systems that no longer meet current Florida Building Code wind resistance standards. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free assessments to help you make an informed decision.

faqs.q: How much does a roof replacement cost in The Acreage, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Costs for a full roof replacement in The Acreage's 33470 zip code typically range from around $12,000 for standard architectural shingles on a mid-sized home to $25,000 or more for larger homes with tile systems. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down for qualified customers, making it easier to move forward without waiting until storm damage forces the issue. Our consultants will provide a detailed written estimate at no charge.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit for a roof replacement in The Acreage, and who handles that?
faqs.a: Yes, a permit is required for any full roof replacement in The Acreage, processed through the Palm Beach County Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — we prepare the documentation, submit the application, and coordinate the final inspection. An unpermitted roof can void your homeowners insurance and complicate a future home sale, so this step is non-negotiable and something we take care of so you don't have to.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take in The Acreage?
faqs.a: Most full roof replacements in The Acreage are completed within one to three days once materials are delivered and the permit is active. The permitting process through Palm Beach County Building Division typically adds one to two weeks to the overall timeline before work begins. Tile roofs may take slightly longer due to material weight and installation complexity. Our team will give you a precise project timeline during your initial consultation and keep you informed throughout every phase of the project.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowners insurance in Palm Beach County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. After your new roof is installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you can request a wind mitigation inspection, and the resulting report — documenting your roof's age, covering, deck attachment, and roof-to-wall connections — qualifies you for mandatory discounts from Florida insurers. Palm Beach County homeowners commonly see annual savings of several hundred to over a thousand dollars. Additionally, replacing an aging roof eliminates surcharges many carriers apply to homes with roofs over 15–20 years old, and helps maintain your insurability long-term.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your The Acreage Home with a New Hurricane-Rated Roof?
cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-obligation roof assessment — our expert team will evaluate your home, walk you through your options, and provide a detailed estimate so you can move forward with confidence before the next storm season.
